proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.pointbiserialr(df['objective_response'], df['ecog_ps'])
# stratified ORR
df.groupby('ecog_ps')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: ORR falls monotonically with ECOG PS: 0=21.3% (n=17592), 1=15.5% (n=24971), 2=11.2% (n=7437). Point-biserial r=-0.094, p=6.4e-99. Highly significant negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with idh1_mutation have higher objective_response rate than patients without idh1_mutation.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
tab = pd.crosstab(df['idh1_mutation'], df['objective_response'])
stats.chi2_contingency(tab)
analyses.result_summary: ORR is 22.13% in IDH1-mutant patients (n=3624) vs 16.50% in IDH1-wildtype (n=46376), absolute difference +5.63 percentage points. Chi-square p=3.5e-18. Strongly significant positive association between IDH1 mutation and response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher bone-marrow blast percentage (blast_pct_marrow) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher peripheral WBC (wbc_k_per_ul) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Point-biserial r between objective_response and marrow blast % is -0.041 (p=1.0e-19). Higher disease burden in marrow predicts lower ORR.
Point-biserial r=-0.019, p=1.8e-5. Higher peripheral WBC at presentation modestly but significantly predicts lower response probability.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with higher objective_response (i.e., better nutritional status predicts response).
Greater weight loss in the prior 6 months (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with lower objective_response (cachexia hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: Point-biserial r=+0.022, p=1.3e-6. Higher albumin levels are significantly associated with higher likelihood of response.
Point-biserial r=-0.055, p=4.6e-35. Greater pre-treatment weight loss is strongly associated with lower response. Cachexia/frailty signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with lower objective_response (systemic inflammation hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: Point-biserial r=-0.021, p=2.9e-6. Higher CRP at baseline is significantly associated with lower response, consistent with the inflammation/poor-prognosis link.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have higher objective_response than those who did not receive it.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 17.35% (n=20020) on ven/aza vs 16.61% (n=29980) off, absolute diff +0.74 pp. Chi-square p=0.031. Modest but statistically significant positive main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among flt3_itd-mutant patients, those treated with treatment_midostaurin have higher objective_response than those not treated with midostaurin (interaction effect).

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = pd.DataFrame({'flt3':df.flt3_itd, 'mido':df.treatment_midostaurin, 'inter':df.flt3_itd*df.treatment_midostaurin})
X = sm.add_constant(X)
m = sm.Logit(df.objective_response, X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: ORR by stratum: FLT3-ITD-/mido- 16.8%, FLT3-ITD-/mido+ 17.0%, FLT3-ITD+/mido- 17.4%, FLT3-ITD+/mido+ 16.1%. Interaction term in logistic model coef=-0.105, p=0.219. No evidence midostaurin specifically benefits FLT3-ITD-mutated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among flt3_itd-mutant patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response (FLT3 inhibitor x FLT3 mutation interaction).
Among flt3_tkd-mutant patients, treatment_midostaurin or treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD x gilteritinib interaction coef=+0.034, p=0.686. Stratum ORR: FLT3-/gilt- 17.0%, FLT3-/gilt+ 16.2%, FLT3+/gilt- 17.2%, FLT3+/gilt+ 16.9%. No interaction; gilteritinib does not preferentially benefit FLT3-ITD patients in this dataset.
FLT3-TKD x midostaurin interaction coef=+0.023, p=0.873. FLT3-TKD x gilteritinib interaction coef=-0.152, p=0.327. Neither FLT3 inhibitor shows a TKD-specific benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among idh1_mutation-positive patients, treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response (targeted therapy hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: Stratum ORR: IDH1-/ivo- 16.4%, IDH1-/ivo+ 17.1%, IDH1+/ivo- 22.5%, IDH1+/ivo+ 17.4%. Interaction term coef=-0.364, p=0.027 (NEGATIVE and statistically significant). Contrary to expectation, IDH1+ patients given ivosidenib actually had numerically lower ORR than IDH1+ patients not given ivosidenib. The expected positive targeted-therapy interaction is not present; the data suggest the opposite direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among idh2_mutation-positive patients, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Stratum ORR: IDH2-/ena- 17.0%, IDH2-/ena+ 16.3%, IDH2+/ena- 16.7%, IDH2+/ena+ 14.4%. Interaction coef=-0.124, p=0.448. No significant biomarker-targeted benefit of enasidenib in IDH2+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among npm1_mutation-positive patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response (NPM1 is known to favor venetoclax response in AML literature).

analyses.result_summary: Stratum ORR: NPM1-/vza- 16.9%, NPM1-/vza+ 16.5%, NPM1+/vza- 16.0%, NPM1+/vza+ 19.3%. Interaction coef=+0.261, p=4.7e-5 (highly significant POSITIVE). NPM1+ patients receiving venetoclax/azacitidine have a meaningfully higher ORR; this is the strongest treatment-by-biomarker interaction in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among tp53_mutation-positive patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has reduced (or no) effect on objective_response (TP53-mutated AML is a known venetoclax-resistance subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: Stratum ORR: TP53-/vza- 16.6%, TP53-/vza+ 17.5%, TP53+/vza- 16.7%, TP53+/vza+ 16.4%. Interaction coef=-0.087, p=0.285. Direction is consistent with TP53 attenuating the ven/aza benefit, but not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among complex_karyotype-positive patients, treatment_7plus3 (intensive induction) has reduced or negative effect on objective_response compared with non-complex-karyotype.

analyses.result_summary: Stratum ORR: CK-/7+3- 16.6%, CK-/7+3+ 17.3%, CK+/7+3- 17.9%, CK+/7+3+ 15.5%. Interaction coef=-0.213, p=0.0016 (significant NEGATIVE). 7+3 chemotherapy is associated with relatively worse response in patients with complex karyotype, consistent with poor cytogenetics resistance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more than in fit patients (low-intensity therapy targeted to unfit).
Among unfit_for_intensive patients, treatment_7plus3 has reduced effect on objective_response compared with fit patients.

analyses.result_summary: Stratum ORR: fit/vza- 16.8%, fit/vza+ 17.0%, unfit/vza- 16.3%, unfit/vza+ 17.9%. Interaction coef=+0.104, p=0.036 (significant POSITIVE). VenAza appears more effective in patients flagged as unfit for intensive therapy.
Interaction coef=-0.033, p=0.503. No statistically detectable differential effect of 7+3 by fitness status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with race_ethnicity == 'black' have lower objective_response than patients with race_ethnicity == 'white'.

analyses.result_summary: Black ORR=15.74% (n=5889) vs White ORR=17.28% (n=32498), absolute diff -1.54 pp. Chi-square p=0.0040. Statistically significant lower response in Black patients in unadjusted comparison; an overall race x ORR test gives chi-square p=0.0096.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) is associated with objective_response.
Rural residence (rural_residence=1) is associated with lower objective_response than urban residence.

analyses.result_summary: ORR: medicare 17.18% (n=20953), medicaid 16.87% (n=6911), private 16.66% (n=20090), uninsured 16.62% (n=2046). Chi-square p~0.55. No significant insurance-based disparity.
Rural ORR=16.91% (n=9996), Urban ORR=16.90% (n=40004), absolute diff <0.001 pp. p~1.0. No urban-rural disparity in response detectable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with different objective_response than male sex.

analyses.result_summary: Female ORR=17.09% (n=22371) vs Male ORR=16.75% (n=27629), diff +0.34 pp. p=0.33. No significant sex difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Carriage of snp_rs1050828 (variant=1) is associated with higher objective_response than non-carriage.

analyses.result_summary: Carriers ORR=18.48% (n=4025) vs non-carriers ORR=16.77% (n=45975), diff +1.72 pp. Chi-square p=0.0056. Statistically significant; only SNP in panel with p<0.01 main effect on ORR after multiple comparisons context (24 SNPs tested).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Atrial fibrillation (atrial_fibrillation=1) is associated with lower objective_response.
Heart failure (heart_failure=1) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: AFib ORR=15.64% (n=4143) vs no-AFib 17.02% (n=45857), diff -1.38 pp. Chi-square p=0.025. Modest but significant negative association.
HF ORR=16.71% (n=5038) vs no-HF 16.93% (n=44962), diff -0.21 pp. p=0.72. No significant association of heart failure with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on age, sex, ECOG PS, key cytogenetic/molecular markers, baseline labs, and treatment indicators, ECOG PS retains a significant negative association and IDH1 mutation retains a significant positive association.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
features = ['age_years','sex_female','ecog_ps','secondary_aml','unfit_for_intensive','complex_karyotype','flt3_itd','flt3_tkd','idh1_mutation','idh2_mutation','npm1_mutation','tp53_mutation','wbc_k_per_ul','blast_pct_marrow','albumin_g_dl','ldh_u_l','weight_loss_pct_6mo','crp_mg_l','nlr','treatment_midostaurin','treatment_gilteritinib','treatment_ivosidenib','treatment_enasidenib','treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine','treatment_7plus3']
X = sm.add_constant(df[features].astype(float))
m = sm.Logit(df.objective_response, X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In adjusted logistic model: ECOG PS coef=-0.384 (p<1e-90), IDH1 coef=+0.360 (p<1e-16), albumin coef=+0.118 (p=9e-7), blast_pct coef=-0.006 (p<1e-19), weight_loss coef=-0.040 (p<1e-33), CRP coef=-0.007 (p<1e-5), WBC coef=-0.002 (p<1e-4), VenAza coef=+0.059 (p=0.016). Age, sex, NPM1, TP53, complex karyotype, FLT3 markers, and individual targeted therapies all non-significant in the adjusted model. Confirms baseline-fitness/disease-burden signature plus an isolated IDH1+ favorable prognostic signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for clinical covariates, race_ethnicity == 'black' remains significantly associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.code:
race_dummies = pd.get_dummies(df.race_ethnicity, prefix='race', drop_first=False).drop(columns=['race_white']).astype(float)
X = pd.concat([df[['age_years','sex_female','ecog_ps','idh1_mutation','npm1_mutation','wbc_k_per_ul','blast_pct_marrow','albumin_g_dl','weight_loss_pct_6mo','crp_mg_l','treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine']], race_dummies], axis=1)
m = sm.Logit(df.objective_response, sm.add_constant(X)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, IDH1, NPM1, WBC, blast %, albumin, weight loss, CRP, and ven/aza, race_black coef = -0.109 (p=0.005); race_other coef = -0.196 (p=0.031); race_asian coef=-0.079 (p=0.13, ns); race_hispanic coef=-0.036 (p=0.30, ns). The Black-vs-White ORR gap persists after adjustment for clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ECOG performance status interacts with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: the ven/aza response benefit differs across ECOG strata.
Age (age_years) interacts with treatment_7plus3: older patients derive less benefit from intensive induction.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG x VenAza interaction coef=-0.026, p=0.48. No evidence ven/aza benefit varies by performance status.
Age x 7+3 interaction coef=+0.0011 (per year), p=0.61. No detectable age modification of 7+3 effect on ORR.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint poor-risk: tp53_mutation and complex_karyotype together (their interaction) lower objective_response beyond their main effects.

analyses.result_summary: TP53 main coef=-0.020 (p=0.65), CK main coef=-0.004 (p=0.92), TP53xCK interaction coef=-0.044 (p=0.70). No evidence of synergistic poor-risk effect on response in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_7plus3 has a positive main effect on objective_response.
Treatment_midostaurin has a positive main effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 17.04% (n=22580) on 7+3 vs 16.79% (n=27420) off, diff +0.25 pp, p=0.50. No significant main effect.
ORR 16.93% (n=7545) on midostaurin vs 16.90% (n=42455) off, diff +0.03 pp. Not significant. Combined with iter 7, midostaurin shows no main effect and no FLT3-ITD-specific effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The IDH1-mutation positive prognostic signal (higher ORR) persists after full multivariable adjustment for treatment, comorbidities, and demographics.
After adjustment, the apparent adverse IDH1 x ivosidenib interaction (iter 9) is attenuated and may be confounded by unmeasured selection bias.

analyses.code:
# Full model adding race + insurance + comorbidities
features = ['age_years','sex_female','ecog_ps','idh1_mutation','idh2_mutation','npm1_mutation','tp53_mutation','complex_karyotype','flt3_itd','wbc_k_per_ul','blast_pct_marrow','albumin_g_dl','weight_loss_pct_6mo','crp_mg_l','atrial_fibrillation','treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine','treatment_7plus3','treatment_ivosidenib']
X = sm.add_constant(df[features].astype(float))
m = sm.Logit(df.objective_response, X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In a fully adjusted model that adds comorbidities and treatments, idh1_mutation coef remains ~+0.36 (p<1e-15) — robust positive prognostic effect. ECOG, weight loss, blast %, albumin, CRP, WBC remain significant in expected directions. VenAza coef ~+0.06 (p~0.02). The IDH1 effect is not explained by treatment selection or measured confounders.

analyses.code:
# Refit IDH1 x ivosidenib interaction in adjusted logistic
features = ['age_years','sex_female','ecog_ps','idh1_mutation','treatment_ivosidenib']
X = df[features].astype(float).copy()
X['idh1_x_ivo'] = df.idh1_mutation * df.treatment_ivosidenib
m = sm.Logit(df.objective_response, sm.add_constant(X)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for age, sex, and ECOG, the IDH1 x ivosidenib interaction coef is approximately -0.36, p~0.03 (essentially unchanged from unadjusted). The negative interaction persists; in this dataset IDH1+ patients given ivosidenib do not gain a response advantage and may even respond less often than IDH1+ patients on other regimens — likely reflects selection bias (sicker IDH1+ patients channeled to ivosidenib) rather than a true harmful drug effect.
